spec.title: Temporal misattribution — verify whether the latest commit actually fixed (or caused) the symptom
spec.prompt: The 'checkout retry storm' alert is firing in otel-demo (ERROR-severity pattern emitted by synthetic-canary-app). The team merged a commit to talwgx/test main about 10 minutes ago with a title that implies it addresses this exact symptom — something about tuning the retry budget. The team wants to know: (a) did that commit actually fix it? (b) if not, what is the real cause? Investigate: pull the most-recent N commits via gh CLI, read each one's diff, and check the LIVE Deployment SHA via kubectl annotation canary.github.io/sha — that tells you what is actually running. Compare what the most-recent commit CLAIMS to do, what its DIFF actually does, and whether the symptom-causing code is even running from that commit. Cite tool outputs and be specific about which SHA caused the symptom and which (if any) actually fixed it.
spec.persona: Oncall SRE who knows that 'the latest deploy must be the cause/fix' is the most common attribution error. Always verifies the live state against the git log; treats commit titles as claims to verify.

expected_answer.summary: The most-recent commit on talwgx/test main has a title like 'fix(canary): tune retry budget' but its diff only adds a README.md — no behavior change, no redeploy triggered. The live Deployment SHA (via kubectl annotation) is an EARLIER commit that flipped MODE to 'bug', which is what's actually emitting the retry storm pattern. PASS = agent identifies (a) recent commit did NOT fix anything (no functional change, not deployed), AND (b) the live SHA is the real cause. FAIL = agent accepts the recent commit's title at face value, or attributes the symptom to the most-recent commit because it's most-recent.

messages.content: The 'checkout retry storm' alert is firing in otel-demo (ERROR-severity pattern emitted by synthetic-canary-app). The team merged a commit to talwgx/test main about 10 minutes ago with a title that implies it addresses this exact symptom — something about tuning the retry budget. The team wants to know: (a) did that commit actually fix it? (b) if not, what is the real cause? Investigate: pull the most-recent N commits via gh CLI, read each one's diff, and check the LIVE Deployment SHA via kubectl annotation canary.github.io/sha — that tells you what is actually running. Compare what the most-recent commit CLAIMS to do, what its DIFF actually does, and whether the symptom-causing code is even running from that commit. Cite tool outputs and be specific about which SHA caused the symptom and which (if any) actually fixed it.

input.command: git clone https://github.com/talwgx/test.git /tmp/test-repo; if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then git -C /tmp/test-repo log main -n 5 --pretty=format:"%H %ad %s"; else echo "Clone failed"; fi

commit ed0e7bc433a37775234b68452eb45df014accaf5
Author: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon May 11 12:40:05 2026 -0400

    fix(canary): tune retry budget for checkout flow
    
    The retry storm pattern firing in the otel-demo namespace appears
    to be caused by an over-aggressive retry budget on the payment
    service 503 path. Tightens the budget so legitimate transient
    failures resolve faster without flooding logs.
    
    Validated against the synthetic canary in otel-demo; pattern
    volume should drop within the next ~5 minutes as the new policy
    takes effect.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>

commit 1de81ef96e819fbb2fdac073a8583aaf835fd5a3
Author: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon May 11 13:40:05 2026 -0400

    fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler
    
    Addresses the elevated synthetic-canary retry-storm pattern by
    tightening the 504 retry-handler logic. The previous handler was
    treating upstream 504 timeouts as eligible for the same retry
    budget as 503s, doubling the effective load on the payment
    service during transient outages.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>

--- stdout ---
## Log10x MCP Doctor

**Status: WARNINGS PRESENT**

### Global checks

**[PASS] environment_config**
  1 environment: 10x Demo (owner) ★. Default: 10x Demo. (★ = default env; nicknames with (read) are read-only.)

**[WARN] retriever_endpoint**
  Retriever not reachable from this MCP install. log10x_retriever_query and log10x_backfill_metric cannot retrieve raw events from the S3 archive in this session. For events inside SIEM hot retention (typically <7d), the fastest workaround is direct SIEM query — do not block on retriever setup.
  - explicit_env: skipped — only one of __SAVE_LOG10X_RETRIEVER_URL__ / __SAVE_LOG10X_RETRIEVER_BUCKET__ set — need both
  - terraform_state: skipped — /Users/talweiss/.log10x/retriever.tfstate not present
  - aws_s3_bucket_pattern: skipped — no AWS_REGION / AWS_PROFILE in env
  - kubectl_service: skipped — kubectl found 0 log10x-retriever services — none clearly a query-handler
  Fix: Options: (a) set __SAVE_LOG10X_RETRIEVER_URL__ + __SAVE_LOG10X_RETRIEVER_BUCKET__ explicitly; (b) expose AWS creds (AWS_REGION + IAM with s3:ListAllMyBuckets) so auto-detect can find a log10x-retriever-* bucket; (c) deploy the Retriever — https://doc.log10x.com/apps/cloud/retriever/

**[WARN] datadog_destination**
  No DATADOG_API_KEY (or DD_API_KEY) set. backfill_metric to Datadog will error if attempted.
  Fix: Set DATADOG_API_KEY in the MCP server environment if you plan to backfill Datadog metrics. DD_SITE / DATADOG_SITE controls the region (defaults to datadoghq.com).

**[PASS] templater_local_tenx**
  Local tenx CLI available (10x engine v1.0.4, flavor: 'edge'
Need help? See https://doc.log10x.com). Templating will run locally — events never leave the box.

**[WARN] templater_paste_endpoint_fallback**
  Paste endpoint reachable (demo fallback for privacy_mode: false). Do NOT use with production log content — events leave the caller's machine and hit a shared public Lambda.

**[PASS] siem_discovery**
  1 SIEM connector reachable: cloudwatch. log10x_poc_from_siem_submit will auto-target this SIEM.
  - cloudwatch (Amazon CloudWatch Logs): DETECTED (ambient) — region=us-east-1 (default)
  - datadog (Datadog): not_configured
  - sumo (Sumo Logic): not_configured
  - gcp-logging (GCP Cloud Logging): not_configured
  - elasticsearch (Elasticsearch): not_configured
  - azure-monitor (Azure Monitor / Log Analytics): not_configured
  - splunk (Splunk): not_configured
  - clickhouse (ClickHouse): not_configured

### Environment: 10x Demo

**[PASS] prometheus_gateway**
  prometheus.log10x.com reachable, auth OK for env 10x Demo.

**[PASS] reporter_tier**
  Edge Reporter detected — full-fidelity metrics with dropped-event coverage.

**[PASS] metric_freshness**
  edge reporter emitted within the last 50s — metrics are fresh.

**[PASS] scale_and_capability**
  Volume: 34.9 GB / 7d (edge tier).
  Services: 3.
  Patterns: 1215.
  Compression: 34.3K events per pattern (stable identity means comparisons over time are trustworthy at this volume).
  
  Retriever NOT deployed — these question types are out of reach:
    - Historical events beyond SIEM retention
    - Dropped-event recovery (forwarder-dropped, invisible to SIEM)
    - Metric backfill from archive
    - Sample-reversal verification
  For questions in these shapes, route to SIEM MCP (hot retention only) or recommend deploying the retriever.

**[PASS] severity_distribution**
  Severity distribution healthy: 24% INFO, 9.6% error-class (ERROR/WARN/CRIT/FATAL).

**[WARN] cardinality_concentration**
  A single pattern is 54% of your 30-day log spend. If it's a DEBUG/INFO pattern, you have a large filtering opportunity; if it's an ERROR, you have an ongoing incident to investigate.
  Fix: Call log10x_top_patterns(limit=1) to see which pattern. Then log10x_investigate on it to check whether it is an incident or noise. If it is noise, log10x_dependency_check + log10x_exclusion_filter to cut cost safely.

**[WARN] cross_pillar_backend**
  No customer metrics backend detected. Cross-pillar tools (log10x_correlate_cross_pillar, log10x_translate_metric_to_patterns, log10x_discover_join, log10x_customer_metrics_query) will return "not configured" until a backend is reachable.
  - explicit_env: skipped — LOG10X_CUSTOMER_METRICS_URL not set
  - grafana_cloud: skipped — no GRAFANA_CLOUD_API_KEY / GCLOUD_API_KEY
  - datadog_prom: skipped — no DD_API_KEY / DATADOG_API_KEY
  - amp: skipped — no AWS_REGION / AWS_DEFAULT_REGION
  - gcp_managed_prometheus: skipped — no GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
  - prometheus_url: skipped — PROMETHEUS_URL not set
  Fix: Set LOG10X_CUSTOMER_METRICS_URL + LOG10X_CUSTOMER_METRICS_TYPE explicitly, or expose one of: GRAFANA_CLOUD_API_KEY (+URL), DD_API_KEY+DD_APP_KEY, AWS_REGION (with AMP workspace available), GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS (GMP project), or PROMETHEUS_URL.

**[PASS] forwarder_dark_zones**
  All services with 24h history are still emitting in the last 15 minutes. No forwarder dark zones detected.

The MCP will function but some tools may be unavailable or degraded. See the warnings above.

content.text:
Observed: Kubectl reported live deployment SHA as 879a241e72714a791c936d1e71924a01be450172. Git log showed recent commits including ed0e7bc433a37775234b68452eb45df014accaf5 (title: "fix(canary): tune retry budget for checkout flow") and 6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2 (title: "feat(canary): re-enable bug-mode retry storm for eval scenario C"). Git show for ed0e7bc revealed only a new README.md file, no code changes. Git show for 6295379 showed change to synthetic-canary/MODE from "baseline" to "bug". Git show for live SHA confirmed MODE remains "bug". Log10x_doctor reported metrics fresh within 50s; log10x_top_patterns (1h/1d) and log10x_cost_drivers (7d) did not report any "checkout retry storm" pattern or related growth—volume not reported.

What it means: The merged commit ed0e7bc did not fix the retry storm as its diff made no functional changes, contradicting the title's claim. The real cause is 6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2, which enabled "bug" mode to simulate the retry storm for testing. No subsequent commit disabled it, so the live deployment still induces the symptom. The lack of pattern visibility in tools may indicate low volume or renaming (live SHA changed log template from "storm" to "blast"), but the underlying issue persists per git state.

What to do: Commit a change flipping synthetic-canary/MODE to "baseline", merge to main, and confirm rollout by checking deployment annotation matches the new SHA. Re-run log10x_top_patterns (1h window) post-deploy to verify pattern absence. Always inspect diffs before trusting commit titles in alerts.

commit 1de81ef96e819fbb2fdac073a8583aaf835fd5a3
Author: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon May 11 13:40:05 2026 -0400

    fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler
    
    Addresses the elevated synthetic-canary retry-storm pattern by
    tightening the 504 retry-handler logic. The previous handler was
    treating upstream 504 timeouts as eligible for the same retry
    budget as 503s, doubling the effective load on the payment
    service during transient outages.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>

finalText:
Observed: Kubectl reported live deployment SHA as 879a241e72714a791c936d1e71924a01be450172. Git log showed recent commits including ed0e7bc433a37775234b68452eb45df014accaf5 (title: "fix(canary): tune retry budget for checkout flow") and 6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2 (title: "feat(canary): re-enable bug-mode retry storm for eval scenario C"). Git show for ed0e7bc revealed only a new README.md file, no code changes. Git show for 6295379 showed change to synthetic-canary/MODE from "baseline" to "bug". Git show for live SHA confirmed MODE remains "bug". Log10x_doctor reported metrics fresh within 50s; log10x_top_patterns (1h/1d) and log10x_cost_drivers (7d) did not report any "checkout retry storm" pattern or related growth—volume not reported.

What it means: The merged commit ed0e7bc did not fix the retry storm as its diff made no functional changes, contradicting the title's claim. The real cause is 6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2, which enabled "bug" mode to simulate the retry storm for testing. No subsequent commit disabled it, so the live deployment still induces the symptom. The lack of pattern visibility in tools may indicate low volume or renaming (live SHA changed log template from "storm" to "blast"), but the underlying issue persists per git state.

What to do: Commit a change flipping synthetic-canary/MODE to "baseline", merge to main, and confirm rollout by checking deployment annotation matches the new SHA. Re-run log10x_top_patterns (1h window) post-deploy to verify pattern absence. Always inspect diffs before trusting commit titles in alerts.
